title: Canto 10 Chapter 44
summary: Complete English Bhagavatam chapter generated from the public gita/Datasets repository. Each route exposes one shloka at a time.
sourceNote: This chapter includes devanagari text, roman transliteration, English translation, and purport when available in the upstream corpus.

verses.text: श्रीशुक उवाचएवं चर्चितसङ्कल्पो भगवान् मधुसूदन: । आससादाथ चाणूरं मुष्टिकं रोहिणीसुत: ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaevaṁ carcita-saṅkalpobhagavān madhusūdanaḥāsasādātha caṇūraṁmuṣṭtikaṁ rohiṇī-sutaḥ
verses.meaning: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Thus addressed, Lord Kṛṣṇa made up His mind to accept the challenge. He paired off with Cāṇūra, and Lord Balarāma with Muṣṭika.
verses.wordMeanings: śrī; śukaḥ; uvāca; evam; carcita; saṅkalpaḥ; bhagavān; madhusūdanaḥ; āsasāda; atha; cāṇūram; muṣṭikam; rohiṇī; sutaḥ

verses.text: हस्ताभ्यां हस्तयोर्बद्ध्वा पद्‌भ्यामेव च पादयो: । विचकर्षतुरन्योन्यं प्रसह्य विजिगीषया ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: hastābhyāṁ hastayor baddhvāpadbhyām eva ca pādayoḥvicakarṣatur anyonyaṁprasahya vijigīṣayā
verses.meaning: Seizing each other’s hands and locking legs with each other, the opponents struggled powerfully, eager for victory.
verses.wordMeanings: hastābhyām; hastayoḥ; baddhvā; padbhyām; eva; ca; pādayoḥ; vicakarṣatuḥ; anyonyam; prasahya; vijigīṣayā

verses.text: अरत्नी द्वे अरत्निभ्यां जानुभ्यां चैव जानुनी । शिर: शीर्ष्णोरसोरस्तावन्योन्यमभिजघ्नतु: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: aratnī dve aratnibhyāṁjānubhyāṁ caiva jānunīśiraḥ śīrṣṇorasoras tāvanyonyam abhijaghnatuḥ
verses.meaning: They each struck fists against fists, knees against knees, head against head and chest against chest.
verses.wordMeanings: aratnī; dve; aratnibhyām; jānubhyām; ca; eva; jānunī; śiraḥ; śīrṣṇā; urasā; uraḥ; tau; anyonyam; abhijaghnatuḥ
verses.purport: The word aratni in this verse may indicate the elbow as well as the fist. Thus blows were perhaps also struck with the elbow, a technique seen today in various martial arts.

verses.text: परिभ्रामणविक्षेपपरिरम्भावपातनै: । उत्सर्पणापसर्पणैश्चान्योन्यं प्रत्यरुन्धताम् ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: paribhrāmaṇa-vikṣepa-parirambhāvapātanaiḥutsarpaṇāpasarpaṇaiścānyonyaṁ pratyarundhatām
verses.meaning: Each fighter contended with his opponent by dragging him about in circles, shoving and crushing him, throwing him down and running before and behind him.
verses.wordMeanings: paribhrāmaṇa; vikṣepa; parirambha; avapātanaiḥ; utsarpaṇa; apasarpaṇaiḥ; ca; anyonyam; pratyarundhatām
verses.purport: Śrīla Śrīdhara Svāmī explains that the word parirambha indicates crushing one’s opponent with one’s arms.

verses.text: उत्थापनैरुन्नयनैश्चालनै: स्थापनैरपि । परस्परं जिगीषन्तावपचक्रतुरात्मन: ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: utthāpanair unnayanaiścālanaiḥ sthāpanair apiparasparaṁ jigīṣantāvapacakratur ātmanaḥ
verses.meaning: Forcefully lifting and carrying each other, pushing each other away and holding each other down, the fighters hurt even their own bodies in their great eagerness for victory.
verses.wordMeanings: utthāpanaiḥ; unnayanaiḥ; cālanaiḥ; sthāpanaiḥ; api; parasparam; jigīṣantau; apacakratuḥ; ātmanaḥ
verses.purport: Śrīla Jīva Gosvāmī explains that although Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma did not, of course, harm Themselves, it appeared that way to Cāṇūra, Muṣṭika and others of mundane vision. In other words, the Lords were fully absorbed in the pastime of being wrestlers.

verses.text: तद् बलाबलवद्युद्धं समेता: सर्वयोषित: । ऊचु: परस्परं राजन् सानुकम्पा वरूथश: ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tad balābalavad yuddhaṁsametāḥ sarva-yoṣitaḥūcuḥ parasparaṁ rājansānukampā varūthaśaḥ
verses.meaning: My dear King, all the women present, considering the match an unfair fight between the strong and the weak, felt extreme anxiety due to compassion. They assembled in groups around the arena and spoke to one another as follows.
verses.wordMeanings: tat; bala; abala; vat; yuddham; sametāḥ; sarva; yoṣitaḥ; ūcuḥ; parasparam; rājan; sa; anukampāḥ; varūthaśaḥ

verses.text: महानयं बताधर्म एषां राजसभासदाम् । ये बलाबलवद्युद्धं राज्ञोऽन्विच्छन्ति पश्यत: ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: mahān ayaṁ batādharmaeṣāṁ rāja-sabhā-sadāmye balābalavad yuddhaṁrājño ’nvicchanti paśyataḥ
verses.meaning: [The women said:] Alas, what a greatly irreligious act the members of this royal assembly are committing! As the King watches this fight between the strong and the weak, they also want to see it.
verses.wordMeanings: mahān; ayam; bata; adharmaḥ; eṣām; rāja; sabhā; sadām; ye; bala; abala; vat; yuddham; rājñaḥ; anvicchanti; paśyataḥ
verses.purport: The idea the ladies are expressing is that even if the King somehow wanted to see such an unfair match, why should the respectable members of the assembly also desire to see it? These feelings are natural. Even nowadays, if in a public place we find a violent fight going on between a very strong, large person and a weaker, smaller person, we are aroused to indignation. Compassionate women are especially offended and enraged by such unfair violence.

verses.text: क्‍व वज्रसारसर्वाङ्गौ मल्लौ शैलेन्द्रसन्निभौ । क्‍व चातिसुकुमाराङ्गौ किशोरौ नाप्तयौवनौ ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: kva vajra-sāra-sarvāṅgaumallau śailendra-sannibhaukva cāti-sukumārāṅgaukiśorau nāpta-yauvanau
verses.meaning: What comparison can there be between these two professional wrestlers, with limbs as strong as lightning bolts and bodies resembling mighty mountains, and these two young, immature boys with exceedingly tender limbs?
verses.wordMeanings: kva; vajra; sāra; sarva; aṅgau; mallau; śaila; indra; sannibhau; kva; ca; ati; su; kumāra; aṅgau; kiśorau; na; āpta; yauvanau

verses.text: धर्मव्यतिक्रमो ह्यस्य समाजस्य ध्रुवं भवेत् । यत्राधर्म: समुत्तिष्ठेन्न स्थेयं तत्र कर्हिचित् ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: dharma-vyatikramo hy asyasamājasya dhruvaṁ bhavetyatrādharmaḥ samuttiṣṭhenna stheyaṁ tatra karhicit
verses.meaning: Religious principles have certainly been violated in this assembly. One should not remain for even a moment in a place where irreligion is flourishing.
verses.wordMeanings: dharma; vyatikramaḥ; hi; asya; samājasya; dhruvam; bhavet; yatra; adharmaḥ; samuttiṣṭhet; na; stheyam; tatra; karhicit

verses.text: न सभां प्रविशेत् प्राज्ञ: सभ्यदोषाननुस्मरन् । अब्रुवन् विब्रुवन्नज्ञो नर: किल्बिषमश्न‍ुते ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: na sabhāṁ praviśet prājñaḥsabhya-doṣān anusmaranabruvan vibruvann ajñonaraḥ kilbiṣam aśnute
verses.meaning: A wise person should not enter an assembly if he knows the participants there are committing acts of impropriety. And if, having entered such an assembly, he fails to speak the truth, speaks falsely or pleads ignorance, he will certainly incur sin.
verses.wordMeanings: na; sabhām; praviśet; prājñaḥ; sabhya; doṣān; anusmaran; abruvan; vibruvan; ajñaḥ; naraḥ; kilbiṣam; aśnute

verses.text: वल्गत: शत्रुमभित: कृष्णस्य वदनाम्बुजम् । वीक्ष्यतां श्रमवार्युप्तं पद्मकोशमिवाम्बुभि: ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: valgataḥ śatrum abhitaḥkṛṣṇasya vadanāmbujamvīkṣyatāṁ śrama-vāry-uptaṁpadma-kośam ivāmbubhiḥ
verses.meaning: Just see the lotus face of Kṛṣṇa as He darts around His foe! That face, covered with drops of perspiration brought on by the strenuous fight, resembles a lotus covered with dew.
verses.wordMeanings: valgataḥ; śatrum; abhitaḥ; kṛṣṇasya; vadana; ambujam; vīkṣyatām; śrama; vāri; uptam; padma; kośam; iva; ambubhiḥ

verses.text: किं न पश्यत रामस्य मुखमाताम्रलोचनम् । मुष्टिकं प्रति सामर्षं हाससंरम्भशोभितम् ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: kiṁ na paśyata rāmasyamukham ātāmra-locanammuṣṭikaṁ prati sāmarṣaṁhāsa-saṁrambha-śobhitam
verses.meaning: Don’t you see the face of Lord Balarāma, with its eyes copper-red from His anger toward Muṣṭika and its beauty enhanced by His laughter and His absorption in the fight?
verses.wordMeanings: kim; na; paśyata; rāmasya; mukham; ātāmra; locanam; muṣṭikam; prati; sa; amarṣam; hāsa; saṁrambha; śobhitam

verses.text: पुण्या बत व्रजभुवो यदयं नृलिङ्ग-गूढ: पुराणपुरुषो वनचित्रमाल्य: । गा: पालयन् सहबल: क्‍वणयंश्च वेणुंविक्रीडयाञ्चति गिरित्ररमार्चिताङ्‍‍‍‍‍घ्रि: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: puṇyā bata vraja-bhuvo yad ayaṁ nṛ-liṅgagūḍhaḥ purāṇa-puruṣo vana-citra-mālyaḥgāḥ pālayan saha-balaḥ kvaṇayaṁś ca veṇuṁvikrīdayāñcati giritra-ramārcitāṅghriḥ
verses.meaning: How pious are the tracts of land in Vraja, for there the primeval Personality of Godhead, disguising Himself with human traits, wanders about, enacting His many pastimes! Adorned with wonderfully variegated forest garlands, He whose feet are worshiped by Lord Śiva and goddess Ramā vibrates His flute as He tends the cows in the company of Balarāma.
verses.wordMeanings: puṇyāḥ; bata; vraja; bhuvaḥ; yat; ayam; nṛ; liṅga; gūḍhaḥ; purāṇa; puruṣaḥ; vana; citra; mālyaḥ; gāḥ; pālayan; saha; balaḥ; kvaṇayan; ca; veṇum; vikrīḍayā; añcati; giritra; ramā; arcita; aṅghriḥ
verses.purport: In this verse the devoted ladies in the audience point out the difference between Mathurā and Vṛndāvana. They want to indicate that in Vṛndāvana Kṛṣṇa simply enjoys with His girlfriends and boyfriends, whereas here in Mathurā the Lord is subjected to harassment by the bullying tactics of professional wrestlers. Thus the ladies are condemning the city of Mathurā because of their pain at seeing Kṛṣṇa in what they consider an unfair wrestling match. Of course, Mathurā is also one of the Lord’s eternal abodes, but here the women in the assembly express their love in a critical mood.

verses.text: गोप्यस्तप: किमचरन् यदमुष्य रूपंलावण्यसारमसमोर्ध्वमनन्यसिद्धम् । द‍ृग्भि: पिबन्त्यनुसवाभिनवं दुराप-मेकान्तधाम यशस: श्रिय ऐश्वरस्य ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: gopyas tapaḥ kim acaran yad amuṣya rūpaṁlāvaṇya-sāram asamordhvam ananya-siddhamdṛgbhiḥ pibanty anusavābhinavaṁ durāpamekānta-dhāma yaśasaḥ śriya aiśvarasya
verses.meaning: What austerities must the gopīs have performed! With their eyes they always drink the nectar of Lord Kṛṣṇa’s form, which is the essence of loveliness and is not to be equaled or surpassed. That form is the only abode of beauty, fame and opulence. It is self-perfect, ever fresh and extremely rare.
verses.wordMeanings: gopyaḥ; tapaḥ; kim; acaran; yat; amuṣya; rūpam; lāvaṇya; sāram; asama; ūrdhvam; ananya; siddham; dṛgbhiḥ; pibanti; anusava; abhinavam; durāpam; ekānta; dhāma; yaśasaḥ; śriyaḥ; aiśvarasya
verses.purport: The word meanings and translation for this verse are from Śrīla Prabhupāda’s Caitanya-caritāmṛta (Ādi 4.156).

verses.text: या दोहनेऽवहनने मथनोपलेप-प्रेङ्खेङ्खनार्भरुदितोक्षणमार्जनादौ । गायन्ति चैनमनुरक्तधियोऽश्रुकण्ठ्योधन्या व्रजस्त्रिय उरुक्रमचित्तयाना: ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: yā dohane ’vahanane mathanopalepapreṅkheṅkhanārbha-ruditokṣaṇa-mārjanādaugāyanti cainam anurakta-dhiyo ’śru-kaṇṭhyodhanyā vraja-striya urukrama-citta-yānāḥ
verses.meaning: The ladies of Vraja are the most fortunate of women because, with their minds fully attached to Kṛṣṇa and their throats always choked up with tears, they constantly sing about Him while milking the cows, winnowing grain, churning butter, gathering cow dung for fuel, riding on swings, taking care of their crying babies, sprinkling the ground with water, cleaning their houses, and so on. By their exalted Kṛṣṇa consciousness they automatically acquire all desirable things.
verses.wordMeanings: yaḥ; dohane; avahanane; mathana; upalepa; preṅkha; iṅkhana; arbha; rudita; ukṣaṇa; mārjana; ādau; gāyanti; ca; enam; anurakta; dhiyaḥ; aśru; kaṇṭhyaḥ; dhanyāḥ; vraja; striyaḥ; urukrama; citta; yānāḥ

verses.text: प्रातर्व्रजाद् व्रजत आविशतश्च सायंगोभि: समं क्‍वणयतोऽस्य निशम्य वेणुम् । निर्गम्य तूर्णमबला: पथि भूरिपुण्या:पश्यन्ति सस्मितमुखं सदयावलोकम् ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: prātar vrajād vrajata āviśataś ca sāyaṁgobhiḥ samaṁ kvaṇayato ’sya niśamya veṇumnirgamya tūrṇam abalāḥ pathi bhūri-puṇyāḥpaśyanti sa-smita-mukhaṁ sa-dayāvalokam
verses.meaning: When the gopīs hear Kṛṣṇa playing His flute as He leaves Vraja in the morning with His cows or returns with them at sunset, the young girls quickly come out of their houses to see Him. They must have performed many pious activities to be able to see Him as He walks on the road, His smiling face mercifully glancing upon them.
verses.wordMeanings: prātaḥ; vrajāt; vrajataḥ; āviśataḥ; ca; sāyam; gobhiḥ; samam; kvaṇayataḥ; asya; niśamya; veṇum; nirgamya; tūrṇam; abalāḥ; pathi; bhūri; puṇyāḥ; paśyanti; sa; smita; mukham; sa; daya; avalokam

verses.text: एवं प्रभाषमाणासु स्त्रीषु योगेश्वरो हरि: । शत्रुं हन्तुं मनश्चक्रे भगवान् भरतर्षभ ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ prabhāṣamāṇāsustrīṣu yogeśvaro hariḥśatruṁ hantuṁ manaś cakrebhagavān bharatarṣabha
verses.meaning: [Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued:] As the women spoke thus, O hero of the Bhāratas, Lord Kṛṣṇa, the master of all mystic power, made up His mind to kill His opponent.
verses.wordMeanings: evam; prabhāṣamāṇāsu; strīṣu; yoga; īśvaraḥ; hariḥ; śatrum; hantum; manaḥ; cakre; bhagavān; bharata; ṛṣabha

verses.text: सभया: स्त्रीगिर: श्रुत्वा पुत्रस्‍नेहशुचातुरौ । पितरावन्वतप्येतां पुत्रयोरबुधौ बलम् ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa-bhayāḥ strī-giraḥ śrutvāputra-sneha-śucāturaupitarāv anvatapyetāṁputrayor abudhau balam
verses.meaning: Out of affection for the two Lords, Their parents [Devakī and Vasudeva] became overwhelmed with sorrow when they heard the women’s fearful statements. They grieved, not knowing their sons’ strength.
verses.wordMeanings: sa; bhayāḥ; strī; giraḥ; śrutvā; putra; sneha; śuca; āturau; pitarau; anvatapyetām; putrayoḥ; abudhau; balam
verses.purport: Naturally, Kṛṣṇa’s parents would lament in this situation, thinking “Why didn’t we keep our sons at home? Why did we allow Them to participate in this corrupt exhibition?”

verses.text: तैस्तैर्नियुद्धविधिभिर्विविधैरच्युतेतरौ । युयुधाते यथान्योन्यं तथैव बलमुष्टिकौ ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tais tair niyuddha-vidhibhirvividhair acyutetarauyuyudhāte yathānyonyaṁtathaiva bala-muṣṭikau
verses.meaning: Lord Balarāma and Muṣṭika, expertly displaying numerous wrestling techniques, battled each other in the same way that Lord Kṛṣṇa and His opponent did.
verses.wordMeanings: taiḥ; taiḥ; niyuddha; vidhibhiḥ; vividhaiḥ; acyuta; itarau; yuyudhāte; yathā; anyonyam; tathā; eva; bala; muṣṭikau

verses.text: भगवद्गात्रनिष्पातैर्वज्रनीष्पेषनिष्ठुरै: । चाणूरो भज्यमानाङ्गो मुहुर्ग्लानिमवाप ह ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: bhagavad-gātra-niṣpātairvajra-nīṣpeṣa-niṣṭhuraiḥcāṇūro bhajyamānāṅgomuhur glānim avāpa ha
verses.meaning: The harsh blows from the Supreme Lord’s limbs fell like crushing lightning bolts upon Cāṇūra, breaking every part of his body and causing him more and more pain and fatigue.
verses.wordMeanings: bhagavat; gātra; niṣpātaiḥ; vajra; niṣpeṣa; niṣṭhuraiḥ; cāṇūraḥ; bhajyamāna; aṅgaḥ; muhuḥ; glānim; avāpa; ha
verses.purport: Cāṇūra’s elbows, arms, knees and other limbs were all weakening.

verses.text: स श्येनवेग उत्पत्य मुष्टीकृत्य करावुभौ । भगवन्तं वासुदेवं क्रुद्धो वक्षस्यबाधत ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa śyena-vega utpatyamuṣṭī-kṛtya karāv ubhaubhagavantaṁ vāsudevaṁkruddho vakṣasy abādhata
verses.meaning: Furious, Cāṇūra attacked Lord Vāsudeva with the speed of a hawk and struck His chest with both fists.
verses.wordMeanings: saḥ; śyena; vegaḥ; utpatya; muṣṭī; kṛtya; karau; ubhau; bhagavantam; vāsudevam; kruddhaḥ; vakṣasi; abādhata
verses.purport: It appears that Cāṇūra, realizing he was being defeated, became furious and made a final attempt to defeat Lord Kṛṣṇa. The demon certainly had the spirit of a good fighter, but if he hoped for victory, he was certainly in the wrong place at the wrong time with the wrong person.

verses.text: नाचलत्तत्प्रहारेण मालाहत इव द्विप: । बाह्वोर्निगृह्य चाणूरं बहुशो भ्रामयन् हरि: ॥ २२ ॥ भूपृष्ठे पोथयामास तरसा क्षीणजीवितम् । विस्रस्ताकल्पकेशस्रगिन्द्रध्वज इवापतत् ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: nācalat tat-prahāreṇamālāhata iva dvipaḥbāhvor nigṛhya cāṇūraṁbahuśo bhrāmayan hariḥ
verses.meaning: No more shaken by the demon’s mighty blows than an elephant struck with a flower garland, Lord Kṛṣṇa grabbed Cāṇūra by his arms, swung him around several times and hurled him onto the ground with great force. His clothes, hair and garland scattering, the wrestler fell down dead, like a huge festival column collapsing.
verses.wordMeanings: na; acalat; tat; prahāreṇa; mālā; āhata; iva; dvipaḥ; bāhvoḥ; nigṛhya; cāṇūram; bahuśaḥ; bhrāmayan; hariḥ; bhū; pṛṣṭhe; pothayām; āsa; tarasā; kṣīṇa; jīvitam; visrasta; ākalpa; keśa; srak; indra; dhvajaḥ; iva; apatat
verses.purport: Śrīla Śrīdhara Svāmī explains the words indra-dhvaja as follows: “In Bengal, on the occasion of a certain festival, people erect a tall column in the form of a man and decorate it with flags, banners, etc. He [Cāṇūra] fell just as such a pole might fall.”

verses.text: तथैव मुष्टिक: पूर्वं स्वमुष्ट्याभिहतेन वै । बलभद्रेण बलिना तलेनाभिहतो भृशम् ॥ २४ ॥ प्रवेपित: स रुधिरमुद्वमन् मुखतोऽर्दित: । व्यसु: पपातोर्व्युपस्थे वाताहत इवाङ्‍‍‍‍‍घ्रिप: ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tathaiva muṣṭikaḥ pūrvaṁsva-muṣṭyābhihatena vaibalabhadreṇa balinātalenābhihato bhṛśam
verses.meaning: Similarly, Muṣṭika struck Lord Balabhadra with his fist and was slain. Receiving a violent blow from the mighty Lord’s palm, the demon trembled all over in great pain, vomited blood and then fell lifeless onto the ground, like a tree blown down by the wind.
verses.wordMeanings: tathā; eva; muṣṭikaḥ; pūrvam; sva; muṣṭyā; abhihatena; vai; balabhadreṇa; balinā; talena; abhihataḥ; bhṛśam; pravepitaḥ; saḥ; rudhiram; udvaman; mukhataḥ; arditaḥ; vyasuḥ; papāta; urvī; upasthe; vāta; āhataḥ; iva; aṅghripaḥ

verses.text: तत: कूटमनुप्राप्तं राम: प्रहरतां वर: । अवधील्लीलया राजन्सावज्ञं वाममुष्टिना ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tataḥ kūṭam anuprāptaṁrāmaḥ praharatāṁ varaḥavadhīl līlayā rājansāvajñaṁ vāma-muṣṭinā
verses.meaning: Confronted next by the wrestler Kūṭa, Lord Balarāma, the best of fighters, playfully and nonchalantly killed him with His left fist, O King.
verses.wordMeanings: tataḥ; kūṭam; anuprāptam; rāmaḥ; praharatām; varaḥ; avadhīt; līlayā; rājan; sa; avajñam; vāma; muṣṭinā

verses.text: तर्ह्येव हि शल: कृष्णप्रपदाहतशीर्षक: । द्विधा विदीर्णस्तोशलक उभावपि निपेततु: ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tarhy eva hi śalaḥ kṛṣṇa-prapadāhata-śīrṣakaḥdvidhā vidīrṇas tośalakaubhāv api nipetatuḥ
verses.meaning: Then Kṛṣṇa kicked in Śala’s head and tore Tośala in half, and both wrestlers fell down dead.
verses.wordMeanings: tarhi; eva; hi; śalaḥ; kṛṣṇa; prapada; āhata; śīrṣakaḥ; dvidhā; vidīrnaḥ; tośalaka; ubhau; api; nipetatuḥ

verses.text: चाणूरे मुष्टिके कूटे शले तोशलके हते । शेषा: प्रदुद्रुवुर्मल्ला: सर्वे प्राणपरीप्सव: ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: cāṇūre muṣṭike kūṭeśale tośalake hateśeṣāḥ pradudruvur mallāḥsarve prāṇa-parīpsavaḥ
verses.meaning: Cāṇūra, Muṣṭika, Kūṭa, Śala and Tośala having been killed, the remaining wrestlers all fled for their lives.
verses.wordMeanings: cāṇūre; muṣṭike; kūṭe; śale; tośalake; hate; śeṣāḥ; pradudruvuḥ; mallāḥ; sarve; prāṇa; parīpsavaḥ

verses.text: गोपान्वयस्यानाकृष्य तै: संसृज्य विजह्रतु: । वाद्यमानेषु तूर्येषु वल्गन्तौ रुतनूपुरौ ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: gopān vayasyān ākṛṣyataiḥ saṁsṛjya vijahratuḥvādyamāneṣu tūryeṣuvalgantau ruta-nūpurau
verses.meaning: Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma then called Their young cowherd boyfriends to join Them, and in their company the Lords danced about and sported, Their ankle bells resounding as musical instruments played.
verses.wordMeanings: gopān; vayasyān; ākṛṣya; taiḥ; saṁsṛjya; vijahratuḥ; vādyamāneṣu; tūryeṣu; valgantau; ruta; nūpurau
verses.purport: Nowadays we see that in championship boxing matches, as soon as there is a victory, all the friends and relatives of the victorious boxer rush into the ring to congratulate him, and often the champion will dance about in great happiness. Exactly in this mood, Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma danced about, celebrating Their victory with Their friends and relatives.

verses.text: जना: प्रजहृषु: सर्वे कर्मणा रामकृष्णयो: । ऋते कंसं विप्रमुख्या: साधव: साधु साध्विति ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: janāḥ prajahṛṣuḥ sarvekarmaṇā rāma-kṛṣṇayoḥṛte kaṁsaṁ vipra-mukhyāḥsādhavaḥ sādhu sādhv iti
verses.meaning: Everyone except Kaṁsa rejoiced at the wonderful feat Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma had performed. The exalted brāhmaṇas and great saints exclaimed, “Excellent! Excellent!”
verses.wordMeanings: janāḥ; prajahṛṣuḥ; sarve; karmaṇā; rāma; kṛṣṇayoḥ; ṛte; kaṁsam; vipra; mukhyāḥ; sādhavaḥ; sādhu; sādhu; iti
verses.purport: It is understood that as the best of the brāhmaṇas and saints were exclaiming “Excellent! Excellent!” the worst of the brāhmaṇas, namely Kaṁsa’s priests, were seriously grieving.

verses.text: हतेषु मल्लवर्येषु विद्रुतेषु च भोजराट् । न्यवारयत् स्वतूर्याणि वाक्यं चेदमुवाच ह ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: hateṣu malla-varyeṣuvidruteṣu ca bhoja-rāṭnyavārayat sva-tūryāṇivākyaṁ cedam uvāca ha
verses.meaning: The Bhoja king, seeing that his best wrestlers had all been killed or had fled, stopped the musical performance originally meant for his pleasure and spoke the following words.
verses.wordMeanings: hateṣu; malla; varyeṣu; vidruteṣu; ca; bhoja; rāṭ; nyavārayat; sva; tūryāṇi; vākyam; ca; idam; uvāca; ha

verses.text: नि:सारयत दुर्वृत्तौ वसुदेवात्मजौ पुरात् । धनं हरत गोपानां नन्दं बध्नीत दुर्मतिम् ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: niḥsārayata durvṛttauvasudevātmajau purātdhanaṁ harata gopānāṁnandaṁ badhnīta durmatim
verses.meaning: [Kaṁsa said:] Drive the two wicked sons of Vasudeva out of the city! Confiscate the cowherds’ property and arrest that fool Nanda!
verses.wordMeanings: niḥsārayata; durvṛttau; vasudeva; ātmajau; purāt; dhanam; harata; gopānām; nandam; badhnīta; durmatim

verses.text: वसुदेवस्तु दुर्मेधा हन्यतामाश्वसत्तम: । उग्रसेन: पिता चापि सानुग: परपक्षग: ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: vasudevas tu durmedhāhanyatām āśv asattamaḥugrasenaḥ pitā cāpisānugaḥ para-pakṣa-gaḥ
verses.meaning: Kill that most evil fool Vasudeva! And also kill my father, Ugrasena, along with his followers, who have all sided with our enemies!
verses.wordMeanings: vasudevaḥ; tu; durmedhā; hanyatām; āśu; asat; tamaḥ; ugrasenaḥ; pitā; ca; api; sa; anugaḥ; para; pakṣa; gaḥ

verses.text: एवं विकत्थमाने वै कंसे प्रकुपितोऽव्यय: । लघिम्नोत्पत्य तरसा मञ्चमुत्तुङ्गमारुहत् ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ vikatthamāne vaikaṁse prakupito ’vyayaḥlaghimnotpatya tarasāmañcam uttuṅgam āruhat
verses.meaning: As Kaṁsa thus raved so audaciously, the infallible Lord Kṛṣṇa, intensely angry, quickly and easily jumped up onto the high royal dais.
verses.wordMeanings: evam; vikatthamāne; vai; kaṁse; prakupitaḥ; avyayaḥ; laghimnā; utpatya; tarasā; mañcam; uttuṅgam; āruhat

verses.text: तमाविशन्तमालोक्य मृत्युमात्मन आसनात् । मनस्वी सहसोत्थाय जगृहे सोऽसिचर्मणी ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tam āviśantam ālokyamṛtyum ātmana āsanātmanasvī sahasotthāyajagṛhe so ’si-carmaṇī
verses.meaning: Seeing Lord Kṛṣṇa approaching like death personified, the quick-witted Kaṁsa instantly rose from his seat and took up his sword and shield.
verses.wordMeanings: tam; āviśantam; ālokya; mṛtyum; ātmanaḥ; āsanāt; manasvī; sahasā; utthāya; jagṛhe; saḥ; asi; carmaṇī

verses.text: तं खड्‌गपाणिं विचरन्तमाशुश्येनं यथा दक्षिणसव्यमम्बरे । समग्रहीद् दुर्विषहोग्रतेजायथोरगं तार्क्ष्यसुत: प्रसह्य ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ khaḍga-pāṇiṁ vicarantam āśuśyenaṁ yathā dakṣiṇa-savyam ambaresamagrahīd durviṣahogra-tejāyathoragaṁ tārkṣya-sutaḥ prasahya
verses.meaning: Sword in hand, Kaṁsa moved quickly from side to side like a hawk in the sky. But Lord Kṛṣṇa, whose fearsome strength is irresistible, powerfully seized the demon just as the son of Tārkṣya might capture a snake.
verses.wordMeanings: tam; khaḍga; pāṇim; vicarantam; āśu; śyenam; yathā; dakṣiṇa; savyam; ambare; samagrahīt; durviṣaha; ugra; tejāḥ; yathā; uragam; tārkṣya; sutaḥ; prasahya

verses.text: प्रगृह्य केशेषु चलत्किरीटंनिपात्य रङ्गोपरि तुङ्गमञ्चात् । तस्योपरिष्टात् स्वयमब्जनाभ:पपात विश्वाश्रय आत्मतन्त्र: ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: pragṛhya keśeṣu calat-kirītaṁnipātya raṅgopari tuṅga-mañcāttasyopariṣṭāt svayam abja-nābhaḥpapāta viśvāśraya ātma-tantraḥ
verses.meaning: Grabbing Kaṁsa by the hair and knocking off his crown, the lotus-naveled Lord threw him off the elevated dais onto the wrestling mat. Then the independent Lord, the support of the entire universe, threw Himself upon the King.
verses.wordMeanings: pragṛhya; keśeṣu; calat; kirīṭam; nipātya; raṅga; upari; tuṅga; mañcāt; tasya; upariṣṭāt; svayam; abja; nābhaḥ; papāta; viśva; āśrayaḥ; ātma; tantraḥ
verses.purport: In Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Śrīla Prabhupāda describes the death of Kaṁsa as follows: “Kṛṣṇa at once straddled his chest and began to strike him over and over again. Simply from the strokes of His fist, Kaṁsa lost his vital force.”

verses.text: तं सम्परेतं विचकर्ष भूमौहरिर्यथेभं जगतो विपश्यत: । हाहेति शब्द: सुमहांस्तदाभू-दुदीरित: सर्वजनैर्नरेन्द्र ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ samparetaṁ vicakarṣa bhūmauharir yathebhaṁ jagato vipaśyataḥhā heti śabdaḥ su-mahāṁs tadābhūdudīritaḥ sarva-janair narendra
verses.meaning: As a lion drags a dead elephant, the Lord then dragged Kaṁsa’s dead body along the ground in full view of everyone present. O King, all the people in the arena tumultuously cried out, “Oh! Oh!”
verses.wordMeanings: tam; samparetam; vicakarṣa; bhūmau; hariḥ; yathā; ibham; jagataḥ; vipaśyataḥ; hā; hā; iti; śabdaḥ; su; mahān; tadā; abhūt; udīritaḥ; sarva; janaiḥ; nara; indra
verses.purport: Śrīla Viśvanātha Cakravartī explains that many people in the audience thought Kaṁsa had simply been knocked unconscious when thrown from the lofty dais. Therefore Lord Kṛṣṇa dragged his corpse so everyone would realize that the evil King was indeed dead. Thus the exclamation hā hā indicates how surprised the people were that the King was suddenly dead and gone. The audience’s astonishment is also mentioned in the Viṣṇu Purāṇa: “Then the entire arena became filled with cries of astonishment as the people saw that the master of Mathurā had been contemptuously killed by Kṛṣṇa.”

verses.text: स नित्यदोद्विग्नधिया तमीश्वरंपिबन्नदन्वा विचरन् स्वपन् श्वसन् । ददर्श चक्रायुधमग्रतो यत-स्तदेव रूपं दुरवापमाप ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa nityadodvigna-dhiyā tam īśvaraṁpibann adan vā vicaran svapan śvasandadarśa cakrāyudham agrato yatastad eva rūpaṁ duravāpam āpa
verses.meaning: Kaṁsa had always been disturbed by the thought that the Supreme Lord was to kill him. Therefore when drinking, eating, moving about, sleeping or simply breathing, the King had always seen the Lord before him with the disc weapon in His hand. Thus Kaṁsa achieved the rare boon of attaining a form like the Lord’s.
verses.wordMeanings: saḥ; nityadā; udvigna; dhiyā; tam; īśvaram; piban; adan; vā; vicaran; svapan; śvasan; dadarśa; cakra; āyudham; agrataḥ; yataḥ; tat; eva; rūpam; duravāpam; āpa
verses.purport: Although born out of fear, Kaṁsa’s constant meditation on the Supreme Lord eradicated all his offenses, and therefore the demon was liberated upon his death at the Lord’s hands.

verses.text: तस्यानुजा भ्रातरोऽष्टौ कङ्कन्यग्रोधकादय: । अभ्यधावन्नतिक्रुद्धा भ्रातुर्निर्वेशकारिण: ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: tasyānujā bhrātaro ’ṣṭaukaṅka-nyagrodhakādayaḥabhyadhāvann ati-kruddhābhrātur nirveśa-kāriṇaḥ
verses.meaning: Kaṁsa’s eight younger brothers, led by Kaṅka and Nyagrodhaka, then attacked the Lords in a rage, seeking to avenge their brother’s death.
verses.wordMeanings: tasya; anujāḥ; bhrātaraḥ; aṣṭau; kaṅka; nyagrodhaka; ādayaḥ; abhyadhāvan; ati; kruddhāḥ; bhrātuḥ; nirveśa; kāriṇaḥ

verses.text: तथातिरभसांस्तांस्तु संयत्तान्‍रोहिणीसुत: । अहन् परिघमुद्यम्य पशूनिव मृगाधिप: ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: tathāti-rabhasāṁs tāṁs tusaṁyattān rohiṇī-sutaḥahan parigham udyamyapaśūn iva mṛgādhipaḥ
verses.meaning: As they ran swiftly toward the two Lords, ready to strike, the son of Rohiṇī slew them with His club just as a lion easily kills other animals.
verses.wordMeanings: tathā; ati; rabhasān; tān; tu; saṁyattān; rohiṇī; sutaḥ; ahan; parigham; udyamya; paśūn; iva; mṛga; adhipaḥ

verses.text: नेदुर्दुन्दुभयो व्योम्नि ब्रह्मेशाद्या विभूतय: । पुष्पै: किरन्तस्तं प्रीता: शशंसुर्ननृतु: स्त्रिय: ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: nedur dundubhayo vyomnibrahmeśādyā vibhūtayaḥpuṣpaiḥ kirantas taṁ prītāḥśaśaṁsur nanṛtuḥ striyaḥ
verses.meaning: Kettledrums resounded in the sky as Brahmā, Śiva and other demigods, the Lord’s expansions, rained down flowers upon Him with pleasure. They chanted His praises, and their wives danced.
verses.wordMeanings: neduḥ; dundubhayaḥ; vyomni; brahma; īśa; ādyāḥ; vibhūtayaḥ; puṣpaiḥ; kirantaḥ; tam; prītāḥ; śaśaṁsuḥ; nanṛtuḥ; striyaḥ

verses.text: तेषां स्त्रियो महाराज सुहृन्मरणदु:खिता: । तत्राभीयुर्विनिघ्नन्त्य: शीर्षाण्यश्रुविलोचना: ॥ ४३ ॥
verses.transliteration: teṣāṁ striyo mahā-rājasuhṛn-maraṇa-duḥkhitāḥtatrābhīyur vinighnantyaḥśīrṣāṇy aśru-vilocanāḥ
verses.meaning: My dear King, the wives of Kaṁsa and his brothers, aggrieved by the death of their well-wishing husbands, came forward with tearful eyes, beating their heads.
verses.wordMeanings: teṣām; striyaḥ; mahārāja; suhṛt; maraṇa; duḥkhitāḥ; tatra; abhīyuḥ; vinighnantyaḥ; śīrṣāṇi; aśru; vilocanāḥ

verses.text: शयानान्वीरशयायां पतीनालिङ्‌‌ग्य शोचती: । विलेपु: सुस्वरं नार्यो विसृजन्त्यो मुहु: शुच: ॥ ४४ ॥
verses.transliteration: śayānān vīra-śayāyāṁpatīn āliṅgya śocatīḥvilepuḥ su-svaraṁ nāryovisṛjantyo muhuḥ śucaḥ
verses.meaning: Embracing their husbands, who lay on a hero’s final bed, the sorrowful women loudly lamented while shedding constant tears.
verses.wordMeanings: śayānān; vīra; śayāyām; patīn; āliṅgya; śocatīḥ; vilepuḥ; su; svaram; nāryaḥ; visṛjantyaḥ; muhuḥ; śucaḥ

verses.text: हा नाथ प्रिय धर्मज्ञ करुणानाथवत्सल । त्वया हतेन निहता वयं ते सगृहप्रजा: ॥ ४५ ॥
verses.transliteration: hā nātha priya dharma-jñakaruṇānātha-vatsalatvayā hatena nihatāvayaṁ te sa-gṛha-prajāḥ
verses.meaning: [The women cried out:] Alas, O master, O dear one, O knower of religious principles! O kind and compassionate protector of the shelterless! By your being slain we have also been slain, together with your household and offspring.
verses.wordMeanings: ha; nātha; priya; dharma; jña; karuṇa; anātha; vatsala; tvayā; hatena; nihatāḥ; vayam; te; sa; gṛha; prajāḥ

verses.text: त्वया विरहिता पत्या पुरीयं पुरुषर्षभ । न शोभते वयमिव निवृत्तोत्सवमङ्गला ॥ ४६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tvayā virahitā patyāpurīyaṁ puruṣarṣabhana śobhate vayam ivanivṛttotsava-maṅgalā
verses.meaning: O great hero among men, bereft of you, its master, this city has lost its beauty, just as we have, and all festivity and good fortune within it have come to an end.
verses.wordMeanings: tvayā; virahitā; patyā; purī; iyam; puruṣa; ṛṣabha; na; śobhate; vayam; iva; nivṛtta; utsava; maṅgalā

verses.text: अनागसां त्वं भूतानां कृतवान्द्रोहमुल्बणम् । तेनेमां भो दशां नीतो भूतध्रुक्को लभेत शम् ॥ ४७ ॥
verses.transliteration: anāgasāṁ tvaṁ bhūtānāṁkṛtavān droham ulbaṇamtenemāṁ bho daśāṁ nītobhūta-dhruk ko labheta śam
verses.meaning: O dear one, you have been brought to this state because of the terrible violence you committed against innocent creatures. How can one who harms others attain happiness?
verses.wordMeanings: anāgasām; tvam; bhūtānām; kṛtavān; droham; ulbaṇam; tena; imām; bho; daśām; nītaḥ; bhūta; dhruk; kaḥ; labheta; śam
verses.purport: Having expressed their sentimental grief, the ladies now speak practical wisdom. They are beginning to see things realistically because their minds were purified by the agony of the recent events and by the association of Lord Kṛṣṇa.

verses.text: सर्वेषामिह भूतानामेष हि प्रभवाप्यय: । गोप्ता च तदवध्यायी न क्‍वचित्सुखमेधते ॥ ४८ ॥
verses.transliteration: sarveṣām iha bhūtānāmeṣa hi prabhavāpyayaḥgoptā ca tad-avadhyāyīna kvacit sukham edhate
verses.meaning: Lord Kṛṣṇa causes the appearance and disappearance of all beings in this world, and He is their maintainer as well. One who disrespects Him can never prosper happily.
verses.wordMeanings: sarveṣām; iha; bhūtānām; eṣaḥ; hi; prabhava; apyayaḥ; goptā; ca; tat; avadhyāyī; na; kvacit; sukham; edhate

verses.text: श्रीशुक उवाचराजयोषित आश्वास्य भगवाँल्लोकभावन: । यामाहुर्लौकिकीं संस्थां हतानां समकारयत् ॥ ४९ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācarāja-yoṣita āśvāsyabhagavāḻ loka-bhāvanaḥyām āhur laukikīṁ saṁsthāṁhatānāṁ samakārayat
verses.meaning: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: After consoling the royal ladies, Lord Kṛṣṇa, sustainer of all the worlds, arranged for the prescribed funeral rites to be performed.
verses.wordMeanings: śrī; śukaḥ; uvāca; rāja; yoṣitaḥ; āśvāsya; bhagavān; loka; bhāvanaḥ; yām; āhuḥ; laukikīm; saṁsthām; hatānām; samakārayat

verses.text: मातरं पितरं चैव मोचयित्वाथ बन्धनात् । कृष्णरामौ ववन्दाते शिरसा स्पृश्य पादयो: ॥ ५० ॥
verses.transliteration: mātaraṁ pitaraṁ caivamocayitvātha bandhanātkṛṣṇa-rāmau vavandāteśirasā spṛśya pādayoḥ
verses.meaning: Then Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma released Their mother and father from bondage and offered obeisances to them, touching their feet with Their heads.
verses.wordMeanings: mātaram; pitaram; ca; eva; mocayitvā; atha; bandhanāt; kṛṣṇa; rāmau; vavandāte; śirasā; spṛśya; pādayoḥ

verses.text: देवकी वसुदेवश्च विज्ञाय जगदीश्वरौ । कृतसंवन्दनौ पुत्रौ सस्वजाते न शङ्कितौ ॥ ५१ ॥नायं श्रियोऽङ्ग उ नितान्तरते: प्रसाद:स्वर्योषितां नलिनगन्धरुचां कुतोऽन्या: ।रासोत्सवेऽस्य भुजदण्डगृहीतकण्ठ-लब्धाशिषां य उदगाद्‍व्रजवल्ल‍भीनाम् ॥
verses.transliteration: devakī vasudevaś cavijñāya jagad-īśvaraukṛta-saṁvandanau putrausasvajāte na śaṅkitau
verses.meaning: Devakī and Vasudeva, now knowing Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma to be the Lords of the universe, simply stood with joined palms. Being apprehensive, they did not embrace their sons.
verses.wordMeanings: devakī; vasudevaḥ; ca; vijñāya; jagat; īśvarau; kṛta; saṁvandanau; putrau; sasvajāte; na; śaṅkitau
